label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking what we know about the mother based on the text. The options are A to D. First, I'll need to go back to the story to recall the details. The mother had five children: Matthew, Marvin, the twins Alisa and Alan, and Helen. She was busy raising them and couldn't grow a garden. She often told her kids that money doesn't grow on trees because they couldn't afford things. Then, after the kids left, on Mother's Day, Marvin planted a money tree with $100 bills and a note saying he owed eight hours of digging. The other kids gave her gardening tools and items. Now she has a beautiful garden and feels her kids are with her when she tends to it. The ending says she's not sure if money grows on trees, but love does. Looking at the options: A. Bad-tempered woman. The text doesn't mention her being angry or bad-tempered. She was strict about money because they couldn't afford things, but that's not necessarily bad-tempered. So probably not A. B. Believes money can grow on a tree now. The last line says she's still not sure about that. Marvin's gift was a joke, but she doesn't actually believe money grows on trees. So B is incorrect. C. She is a writer. There's no mention of her writing anything. The story is told in first person, but that doesn't mean she's a writer. The text doesn't state she writes professionally. So C is likely wrong. D. Hard-working in housekeeping. She
